data.name: AppTagline
data.value: Understand what is happening on your PC, without telemetry

data.name: ChooseCheckTitle
data.value: What would you like to check?

data.name: ChooseCheckSubtitle
data.value: Choose a check. The overview is recommended to get started.

data.name: FlaggedOnly
data.value: Show only items that need attention

data.name: FlaggedOnlyTooltip
data.value: Hides normal items to reduce noise.

data.name: ReadySummary
data.value: Ready. Select an analysis, then click Start.

data.name: DefaultGuidance
data.value: An alert is a clue, not proof of infection.

data.name: ReadOnlyFooter
data.value: Read-only: WinSight never deletes or blocks automatically. VirusTotal stays disabled without WINSIGHT_VT_KEY.

data.name: TrayHintMessage
data.value: WinSight is still running in the notification area.

data.name: ScanCancelledSummary
data.value: Analysis stopped safely between two steps.

data.name: InsufficientSummary
data.value: Insufficient permissions. This analysis may require running as administrator.

data.name: ProgressInsufficient
data.value: Analysis cannot run with the current permissions.

data.name: ReadingWindows
data.value: WinSight is reading Windows information. No changes are being made.

data.name: StopRequested
data.value: Stop requested — the current step will finish without being interrupted abruptly…

data.name: NoItems
data.value: No items match the current filter. This does not guarantee that the system is threat-free.

data.name: SelectFinding
data.value: Select a row to see the safe actions available.

data.name: ToolOverviewDescription
data.value: Checks the main signals: startup items, camera/microphone, connections, DNS, extensions, hosts and certificates.

data.name: ToolOverviewGuidance
data.value: Start with “Needs attention” rows. An alert is a clue: check the name, location and publisher before taking action.

data.name: ToolPersistenceDescription
data.value: Finds programs, services and settings that can return at every startup or sign-in.

data.name: ToolPersistenceGuidance
data.value: An unknown or unsigned item deserves review. Never remove a system entry without a backup and confirmation.

data.name: ToolAvDescription
data.value: Shows apps recorded by Windows as having used the webcam or microphone, including those active now.

data.name: ToolAvGuidance
data.value: If a sensor is active unexpectedly, close the app and review its permissions in Windows privacy settings.

data.name: ToolNetworkDescription
data.value: Links TCP/UDP connections to the programs that opened them and to their digital signatures.

data.name: ToolNetworkGuidance
data.value: An external connection is not necessarily malicious. First check the program, its path and its signature.

data.name: ToolDnsDescription
data.value: Shows the Windows DNS cache: domain names recently resolved by this computer.

data.name: ToolDnsGuidance
data.value: A DNS entry records a lookup, not necessarily an intentional visit. Correlate it with connections and active programs.

data.name: ToolFirewallDescription
data.value: Inventories Microsoft Defender Firewall rules. Automatic blocking stays disabled until the secured WFP service is delivered.

data.name: ToolFirewallGuidance
data.value: Use the Windows tool to change a rule. Keep a recovery path before blocking a system service.

data.name: ToolProcessesDescription
data.value: Lists processes, their paths, parents, command lines and signatures.

data.name: ToolProcessesGuidance
data.value: An unsigned process may be legitimate. Open its location and confirm its origin before ending it.

data.name: ToolModulesDescription
data.value: Finds unsigned or untrusted DLLs loaded into accessible processes.

data.name: ToolModulesGuidance
data.value: This check can take time. An unknown DLL inside a signed program warrants investigation, not immediate deletion.

data.name: ToolExtensionsDescription
data.value: Inspects Chromium extensions and flags permissions that can read or change many pages.

data.name: ToolExtensionsGuidance
data.value: Disable unnecessary or unknown extensions in the browser, then verify their publisher and official listing.

data.name: ToolCertificatesDescription
data.value: Finds root certificates with risky signals: a local private key, a weak signature or a key that is too short.

data.name: ToolCertificatesGuidance
data.value: Never remove an enterprise certificate without your administrator’s advice. Compare the issuer and expected use.

data.name: ToolHostsDescription
data.value: Detects redirects that may hijack a website or block security and update services.

data.name: ToolHostsGuidance
data.value: Ad blockers also use this file. Check the domain and address before changing anything.
